Although serialization for <general-enclosed> is not\n    16→  // specified[1], unknown function names are unlikely to be resolved to be\n    17→  // serialized lower-case. Also, keeping the case is currently interoperable.\n    18→  //\n    19→  // [1] https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/7266\n    20→  sheet.replaceSync('@container STYLE(--foo: bar){}');\n    21→  assert_implements(sheet.cssRules[0].containerQuery === \"style(--foo: bar)\",\n    22→                    \"Basic support for style container queries required\");\n    23→}\n    24→\n    25→function cleanup_container_query_main() {\n    26→  const main = document.querySelector(\"#cq-main\");\n    27→  while (main.firstChild)\n    28→    main.firstChild.remove();\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→function set_container_query_style(text) {\n    32→  let style = document.createElement('style');\n    33→  style.innerText = text;\n    34→  document.querySelector(\"#cq-main\").append(style);\n    35→  return style;\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→function test_cq_rule_invalid(query) {\n    39→  const ruleText = `@container ${query} {}`;\n    40→  test(t => {\n    41→    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    42→    let style = set_container_query_style(ruleText);\n    43→    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 0);\n    44→  }, `@container rule should be invalid: ${ruleText} {}`);\n    45→}\n    46→\n    47→function test_cq_rule_valid(query) {\n    48→  const ruleText = `@container ${query} {}`;\n    49→  test(t => {\n    50→    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    51→    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${query} {}`);\n    52→    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 1);\n    53→  }, `@container rule should be valid: ${ruleText} {}`);\n    54→}\n    55→\n    56→function test_cq_condition_invalid(condition) {\n    57→  test(t => {\n    58→    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    59→    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container name ${condition} {}`);\n    60→    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 0);\n    61→  }, `Query condition should be invalid: ${condition}`);\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→// Tests that 1) the condition parses, and 2) is either \"unknown\" or not, as\n    65→// specified.\n    66→function test_cq_condition_valid(condition, unknown) {\n    67→  test(t => {\n    68→    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    69→    let style = set_container_query_style(`\n    70→      @container name ${condition} {}\n    71→      @container name (${condition}) or (not (${condition})) { main { --match:true; } }\n    72→    `);\n    73→    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, sure to update the match in as_specific when this is updated\n        match rule {\n            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSImportRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSStyleRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSFontFaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSKeyframesRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSMediaRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSNamespaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSSupportsRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerBlockRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerStatementRule::new(\n                window,\n                parent_stylesheet,\n                s,\n                can_gc,\n            )),\n            StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Property(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSPropertyRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSNestedDeclarations::new(\n                window,\n                parent_stylesheet,\n                s,\n                can_gc,\n            )),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Sets owner sheet/rule to null\n    pub(crate) fn detach(&self) {\n        self.deparent();\n        // should set parent rule to None when we add parent rule support\n    }\n\n    /// Sets owner sheet to null (and does the same for all children)\n    pub(crate) fn deparent(&self) {\n        self.parent_stylesheet_removed.set(true);\n        // https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/722\n        // Spec doesn't ask us to do this, but it makes sense\n        // and browsers implement this behavior\n        self.as_specific().deparent_children();\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\n        &self.parent_stylesheet\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n        self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, style_rule: &StyleCssRule, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\n        match style_rule {\n            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":277}}}

self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else {\n            unreachable!()\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Given a StyleCssRule, create a new instance of a derived class of\n    // CSSRule based on which rule it is\n    pub(crate) fn new_specific(\n        window: &Window,\n        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n        rule: StyleCssRule,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> DomRoot<CSSRule> {\n        // be unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerBlockRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerStatementRule::new(\n unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSNestedDeclarations::new(\n detach(&self) {\n        self.deparent();\n        // should set parent rule to None when we add parent rule support\n    }\n\n    /// Sets owner sheet to null (and does the same for all children)\n    pub(crate) fn deparent(&self) {\n        self.parent_stylesheet_removed.set(true);\n        // https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/722\n        // Spec doesn't ask us to do this, but it makes sense\n        // and browsers implement this behavior\n        self.as_specific().deparent_children();\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\n        &self.parent_stylesheet\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n        self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, style_rule:               }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.\n\n## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel\n\nUse the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.\n\n### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review\n\nFor each change:\n\n1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Use Grep to find similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.\n2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.\n3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.\n\n### Agent 2: Code Quality Review\n\nReview the same changes for hacky patterns:\n\n1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls\n2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones\n3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction\n4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries\n5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase\n\n### Agent 3: Efficiency Review\n\nReview the same changes for efficiency:\n\n1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns\n2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel\n3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths\n4. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error\n5. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks\n6. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one\n\n## Phase 3: Fix Issues\n\nWait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. self.global().as_window(),\n    70→                parent_stylesheet,\n    71→                RulesSource::Keyframes(self.keyframesrule.borrow().clone()),\n    72→                can_gc,\n    73→            )\n    74→        })\n    75→    }\n    76→\n    77→    /// Given a keyframe selector, finds the index of the first corresponding rule if any\n    78→    fn find_rule(&self, selector: &DOMString) -> Option<usize> {\n    79→        let selector = selector.str();\n    80→        let mut input = ParserInput::new(&selector);\n    81→        let mut input = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    82→        if let Ok(sel) = KeyframeSelector::parse(&mut input) {\n    83→            let guard = self.cssrule.shared_lock().read();\n    84→            // This finds the *last* element matching a selector\n    85→            // because that's the rule that applies.    91→                .rposition(|frame| frame.read_with(&guard).selector == sel)\n    92→        } else {\n    93→            None\n    94→        }\n    95→    }\n    96→\n    97→    pub(crate) fn update_rule(\n    98→        &self,\n    99→        keyframesrule: Arc<Locked<KeyframesRule>>,\n   100→        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n   101→    ) {\n   102→        if let Some(rulelist) = The inheritance hierarchy (CSSConditionRule → CSSGroupingRule → CSSRule) and the pattern of storing a RefCell'd Arc of the Stylo rule are appropriate for the Servo architecture.\n\n**However, duplication exists at multiple levels:**\n\n1. **Struct boilerplate**: The new_inherited() and new() patterns repeat ~40 lines across each condition rule type\n2. **Trait implementation**: The SpecificCSSRule impl is ~10 identical lines per type\n3. **Dispatch chains**: Two separate dispatch locations (cssrule.rs and cssconditionrule.rs) could potentially be consolidated\n\n### RECOMMENDATIONS\n\n1. **Macro for rule creation** (low-hanging fruit): Create a macro to generate `new_inherited()` and `new()` methods to eliminate 30+ lines of duplication per condition rule:\n   ```rust\n   macro_rules! impl_condition_rule_new {\n       ($rule_type:ty, $stylo_type:ty, $field_name:ident) => {\n           impl $rule_type {\n               fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet, rule: Arc<$stylo_type>) -> Self {\n                   let list = rule.rules.clone();\n                   Self {\n                       cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, list),\n                       $field_name: RefCell::new(rule),\n                   }\n               }\n               // ... new() method\n           }\n       }\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Trait-based dispatch in cssrule.rs**: Use a trait method approach instead of explicit downcasts in `as_specific()`. However, this creates a specialized method that differs from `CSSMediaRule::get_condition_text()` (line 68-76) and `CSSSupportsRule::get_condition_text()` (line 59-61), which only use the condition.\n\n**The Issue:** This is container-query-specific behavior that should ideally be encapsulated at the style layer. If the style `ContainerRule` struct already has a method to serialize the full condition text with the name, this DOM layer code duplicates that logic.\n\n**Concern:** Maintenance risk - if container query condition serialization changes in the style layer, this code won't automatically track those changes.\n\n---\n\n### **ISSUE: Dispatch Chain Fragility - Hardcoded Type Checks**\n\n**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Location:** `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssconditionrule.rs` lines 64-74\n\n**Problem:**\nThe `ConditionText()` method uses explicit downcast checks to determine rule type:\n\n```rust\nfn ConditionText(&self) -> DOMString {\n    if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n        rule.get_condition_text()\n    } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n        rule.get_condition_text()\n    } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSContainerRule>() {\n        rule.get_condition_text()\n    } else {\n        unreachable!()\n    }\n}\n```\n\nAnd in `cssrule.rs`, there are two large match statements (lines 94-147 and 174-245) that explicitly handle every rule type.\n\n**The Issue:** This is a fragile dispatch pattern because:\n1. Each new rule type requires updates to multiple locations\n2. The comment at line 93 in cssrule.rs says \"be sure to update the match in as_specific when this is updated\" - requiring manual synchronization\n3. The `unreachable!()` at line 81 in cssrule.rs assumes complete coverage but provides no safety mechanism\n4. Any new condition rule type must be added to `cssconditionrule.rs` manually\n\n**Better Pattern:** Use a trait method like `get_condition_text()` in the `SpecificCSSRule` trait instead of hardcoded dispatches. This would reduce code duplication and provide compile-time safety.\n\n---\n\n### **ISSUE: Redundant State - Containerrule RefCell**\n\n**Severity:** LOW | **Location:** `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/csscontainerrule.rs` line 28\n\n**Problem:**\nThe `containerrule` field is stored as:\n```rust\n#[no_trace]\ncontainerrule: RefCell<Arc<ContainerRule>>,\n```\n\nThis is identical to the pattern in `CSSMediaRule` (line 29) and `CSSSupportsRule` (line 27), which also use `RefCell<Arc<Rule>>`. However, the state is somewhat redundant because:\n\n1. The `Arc<ContainerRule>` is already immutable (can't mutate through an `Arc`)\n2. The `RefCell` only allows replacing the entire rule, not mutating it in-place\n3. The rules field inside `CSSConditionRule` also stores the rules from the same rule object\n\n**Minor Concern:** This pattern is consistent with media/supports rules, so it's not a deviation. However, examining `CSSSupportsRule::get_condition_text()` (line 59-61), it reads the guard and immediately uses it:\n\n```rust\npub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\n    self.supportsrule.borrow().condition.to_css_string().into()\n}\n```\n\n**Wait - Correction:** Actually, looking more carefully, `CSSSupportsRule` doesn't even acquire the guard! This suggests that for simple value reads like `to_css_string()`, the guard may not be strictly necessary. However, `CSSMediaRule::get_condition_text()` (line 68-76) DOES acquire the guard before reading from the locked structure:\n\n```rust\nlet guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\nself.mediarule\n    .borrow()\n    .media_queries\n    .read_with(&guard)  // <-- Guard is used here\n    .to_css_string()\n    .into()\n```\n\n**The Real Issue:** `CSSContainerRule::get_condition_text()` acquires a guard but **doesn't use it**. The `rule.condition` field is accessed directly without `read_with(&guard)`. If `condition` is a locked field, this could cause data races.\n\n---\n\n### **ISSUE: Inconsistent API - ContainerName() Returns Cloned String**\n\n**Severity:** LOW | **Location:** `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/csscontainerrule.rs` line 87\n\n**Problem:**\n```rust\nfn ContainerName(&self) -> DOMString {\n    self.containerrule.borrow().name.clone().into()\n}\n```\n\nThis clones the name field. Comparing with other rule types, this is consistent with the pattern (all string fields get cloned when returned). However, this is inefficient for what should be a simple value read. The `.clone()` is necessary because `name` is owned data that must be converted to a DOM string.\n\n**Minor Note:** This is not really a bug, but it's worth documenting that every DOM property access involves cloning. For large container names, this could be optimized with Copy-on-Write or similar patterns, though this is likely a pre-existing pattern in the codebase.\n\n---\n\n### **Summary Table**\n\n| Issue | Severity | Type | File(s) |\n|-------|----------|------|---------|\n| Missing `deparent_children()` implementation | **HIGH** | Logic Error | csscontainerrule.rs |\n| Copy-paste condition text logic | MEDIUM | Code Duplication | csscontainerrule.rs |\n| Hardcoded dispatch chains | MEDIUM | Maintenance Risk | cssrule.rs, cssconditionrule.rs |\n| Guard acquired but not used | MEDIUM | Potential Data Race | csscontainerrule.rs:54-64 |\n| Redundant RefCell wrapper | LOW | Minor Design Issue | csscontainerrule.rs |\n| Inefficient string cloning | LOW | Performance | csscontainerrule.rs:87 |\n\n---\n\n### **Recommendations**\n\n1. **Add deparent_children() implementation** to ensure child rules are properly detached from the stylesheet\n2. **Review the lock guard usage** in `get_condition_text()` to ensure thread safety is maintained\n3. **Consider refactoring dispatch patterns** to use trait methods instead of hardcoded type checks\n4. **Add unit tests** specifically for deparenting behavior with container rules containing nested rules"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a4d7a1457a8812266 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 35523\ntool_uses: 20\nduration_ms: 45522</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2dd9c3a0-6f90-4203-800d-f2de96cc6ff0","uuid":"5e921d18-8fbc-4481-bd68-9fe739001f1c","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"You are reviewing CSS rule implementation changes for code quality issues.\n\n## Changes Summary\nImplementing CSSContainerRule - Comparing with other rule types, this is consistent with the pattern (all string fields get cloned when returned). However, this is inefficient for what should be a simple value read. The `.clone()` is necessary because `name` is owned data that must be converted to a DOM string.\n\n**Minor Note:** This is not really a bug, but it's worth documenting that every DOM property access involves cloning. For large container names, this could be optimized with Copy-on-Write or similar patterns, though this is likely a pre-existing pattern in the codebase.\n\n---\n\n### **Summary Table**\n\n| Issue | Severity | Type | File(s) |\n|-------|----------|------|---------|\n| Missing `deparent_children()` implementation | **HIGH** | Logic Error | csscontainerrule.rs |\n| Copy-paste condition text logic | MEDIUM | Code Duplication | csscontainerrule.rs |\n| Hardcoded dispatch chains | MEDIUM | Maintenance Risk | cssrule.rs, cssconditionrule.rs |\n| Guard acquired but not used | MEDIUM | Potential Data Race | csscontainerrule.rs:54-64 |\n| Redundant RefCell wrapper | LOW | Minor Design Issue | csscontainerrule.rs |\n| Inefficient string cloning | LOW | Performance | csscontainerrule.rs:87 |\n\n---\n\n### **Recommendations**\n\n1. **Add deparent_children() implementation** to ensure child rules are properly detached from the stylesheet\n2. **Review the lock guard usage** in `get_condition_text()` to ensure thread safety is maintained\n3. **Consider refactoring dispatch patterns** to use trait methods instead of hardcoded type checks\n4. **Add unit tests** specifically for deparenting behavior with container rules containing nested rules"}],"totalDurationMs":45522,"totalTokens":35523,"totalToolUseCount":20,"usage":{"input_tokens":4,"cache_creation_input_tokens":486,"cache_read_input_tokens":32440,"output_tokens":2593,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":486,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

This needs to be standardized across all three rule types.\n2. Consolidate duplicated condition text logic that appears across container, media, and supports rules\n3. Eliminate hardcoded dispatch chains that require manual maintenance\n\n### LOW Priority (Nice to Have):\n1. Reduce string allocations in get_condition_text() - use a builder or write to buffer\n2. Better dispatch mechanism (but this is pre-existing issue)\n3. Macros for duplicated code (but this is architectural improvement for later)\n\n---\n\nLooking at the actual implementations reveals the lock inconsistency. CSSMediaRule properly acquires and uses the guard with `read_with()` since its media_queries field is `Locked<T>`. CSSSupportsRule takes a different approach, skipping the guard entirely because condition isn't wrapped in a lock. CSSContainerRule acquires a guard but never applies it, suggesting either the field doesn't need locking or the implementation is incomplete.\n\nThe pattern indicates that if a field were `Locked<T>`, it would require `read_with(&guard)` like the media rule does. Since ContainerRule's condition is accessed directly with `.to_css_string()`, the guard acquisition appears unnecessary. The same issue appears in ContainerQuery(), where a guard is obtained but unused.\n\nFor `deparent_children()`, I need to understand when this is actually required. The SpecificCSSRule trait provides a default no-op implementation, with only CSSKeyframesRule overriding it due to its inline keyframes, and CSSMediaRule handling it through CSSConditionRule's rule collection. Looking at the actual implementations shows most rules don't need custom deparenting logic. the CSSRuleList. So when a rule is deparented, we don't need to deparent child rules because they're Stylo-level rules, not DOM-level rules.\n\nLooking at how CSSRuleList creates DOM rules on-demand, when a rule gets deparented from the stylesheet, its child rules become orphaned as well. CSSKeyframesRule explicitly implements deparent_children() to handle this—it iterates through keyframe rules and deparents each one.\n\nThe real question is whether CSSContainerRule needs the same treatment. Checking the codebase, CSSConditionRule (the parent class) doesn't implement deparent_children(), and neither do CSSMediaRule or CSSSupportsRule—they all use the default no-op. This suggests it might not be necessary for CSSContainerRule either, though the concern about cached child DOM rules is worth considering. The architecture seems to handle this differently than CSSKeyframesRule does.\n\nThe child rules are created on-demand from Stylo rules in the CSSRuleList rather than being cached directly in the rule object, so explicit deparenting likely isn't required here.\n\nThe main fixes needed are removing the unused lock acquisitions in ContainerQuery() and determining whether the guard in get_condition_text() is actually necessary. I should also verify this matches the pattern used in CSSSupportsRule. Looking at the inconsistency across these CSS rule types, CSSMediaRule acquires and uses a guard with read_with(), while CSSSupportsRule skips the guard entirely. CSSContainerRule currently acquires a guard but never uses it, which breaks the pattern.\n\nThe condition field likely isn't wrapped in Locked<T> based on the direct .to_css_string() calls in the code, so I should follow CSSSupportsRule's approach and remove the unnecessary guard acquisitions from both ContainerQuery() and get_condition_text(). The deparent_children() implementation and string allocation optimizations can wait—the guard removal is the priority fix.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":11478,"cache_read_input_tokens":82957,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":11478},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2dd9c3a0-6f90-4203-800d-f2de96cc6ff0","uuid":"eb1a0626-4b28-4997-9f95-164d63c77c86"}
